_schema.description: Population pyramid data for four countries. Each dataset contains age bands with female and male counts in thousands. Replace with real census data.

fields.datasets[key].label: string — displayed as chart subtitle
fields.datasets[key].shape: string — shape classification: Expansive, Constrictive, Stationary, Transitional, Inverted
fields.datasets[key].shapeNote: string — one-line interpretation of the shape (shown as badge tooltip)
fields.datasets[key].data[].band: string — age band label (ordered youngest to oldest)

_schema.replace: Swap any dataset's data array with real census values. Keep band labels consistent across datasets for the overlay comparison to work correctly. Shape and shapeNote are interpretive — update them to match your data's actual demographic structure.

nigeria.label: Nigeria (2023)
nigeria.shapeNote: Wide base, high fertility and mortality — rapid population growth

germany.label: Germany (2023)
germany.shapeNote: Narrow base, ageing population — shrinking workforce, pension pressure

india.label: India (2023)
india.shapeNote: Bulging working-age cohorts — demographic dividend window

japan.label: Japan (2023)
japan.shapeNote: Narrowest base — world's most acute demographic decline
